Functions for managing Bluetooth pairability and discoverability in BT Classic and BLE modes.

# Purpose
This C header file defines a set of functions for managing Bluetooth (BT) pairability in a system. The functions allow the system to be discovered and paired with over Bluetooth Classic and Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) using reference counting. Functions such as [`bt_pairability_use`](<#bt_pairability_use>), [`bt_pairability_use_bt`](<#bt_pairability_use_bt>), and [`bt_pairability_use_ble`](<#bt_pairability_use_ble>) enable pairability, while [`bt_pairability_release`](<#bt_pairability_release>), [`bt_pairability_release_bt`](<#bt_pairability_release_bt>), and [`bt_pairability_release_ble`](<#bt_pairability_release_ble>) disable it. The function [`bt_pairability_use_ble_for_period`](<#bt_pairability_use_ble_for_period>) allows BLE pairability for a specified duration, after which it automatically calls [`bt_pairability_release_ble`](<#bt_pairability_release_ble>). The function [`bt_pairability_update_due_to_bonding_change`](<#bt_pairability_update_due_to_bonding_change>) checks for existing bondings to gateways and makes the system discoverable and pairable if none exist. The [`bt_pairability_init`](<#bt_pairability_init>) function initializes the pairability management system.
# Function Declarations (Public API)

---
### bt\_pairability\_use<!-- {{#callable_declaration:bt_pairability_use}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/common/bluetooth/pairability.h#L20>)

Requests to allow Bluetooth Classic and LE pairing.
- **Description**: Use this function to make the system discoverable and pairable over both Bluetooth Classic and Bluetooth Low Energy (LE). It increases the internal reference count for pairing requests, which helps manage multiple concurrent requests. This function must be called when you want to enable pairing capabilities, and it is important to ensure that corresponding release functions are called to maintain proper reference count balance.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`bt_pairability_use`](<pairability.c.md#bt_pairability_use>)  (Implementation)


---
### bt\_pairability\_use\_bt<!-- {{#callable_declaration:bt_pairability_use_bt}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/common/bluetooth/pairability.h#L23>)

Requests Bluetooth Classic discoverability and pairability.
- **Description**: Use this function to make the system discoverable and pairable over Bluetooth Classic. It increases an internal reference count to track active requests for Bluetooth Classic pairing. This function is part of a reference counting mechanism, so it must be paired with a corresponding call to `bt_pairability_release_bt` to decrease the reference count when pairing is no longer needed. Ensure that the system is initialized before calling this function.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`bt_pairability_use_bt`](<pairability.c.md#bt_pairability_use_bt>)  (Implementation)


---
### bt\_pairability\_use\_ble<!-- {{#callable_declaration:bt_pairability_use_ble}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/common/bluetooth/pairability.h#L26>)

Requests to allow BLE pairing.
- **Description**: Use this function to enable the system to be discoverable and pairable over Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE). This function increases an internal reference count, which tracks the number of active requests for BLE pairability. It must be paired with a corresponding call to `bt_pairability_release_ble` to decrease the reference count when BLE pairability is no longer needed. This function does not take any parameters and does not return a value.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`bt_pairability_use_ble`](<pairability.c.md#bt_pairability_use_ble>)  (Implementation)


---
### bt\_pairability\_use\_ble\_for\_period<!-- {{#callable_declaration:bt_pairability_use_ble_for_period}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/common/bluetooth/pairability.h#L30>)

Enables BLE pairability for a specified duration.
- **Description**: Use this function to make the system discoverable and pairable over Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) for a specified number of seconds. It is useful when temporary BLE pairability is needed. The function ensures that BLE pairability is activated if it is not already scheduled, and it always reschedules the timer with the new duration, even if it is shorter than a previously set duration. This function should be called when you want to control the period during which the system is pairable over BLE.
- **Inputs**:
    - `duration_secs`: Specifies the duration in seconds for which BLE pairability should be enabled. Must be a positive integer. The function will handle the scheduling of the timer based on this duration.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`bt_pairability_use_ble_for_period`](<pairability.c.md#bt_pairability_use_ble_for_period>)  (Implementation)


---
### bt\_pairability\_release<!-- {{#callable_declaration:bt_pairability_release}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/common/bluetooth/pairability.h#L33>)

Decreases the reference count for Bluetooth pairability.
- **Description**: Use this function to decrease the reference count for Bluetooth Classic and BLE pairability, which may affect the device's discoverability and ability to pair. This function should be called when you no longer need the device to be discoverable or pairable over Bluetooth. Ensure that the reference count is not zero before calling this function, as it asserts that the count is non-zero.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`bt_pairability_release`](<pairability.c.md#bt_pairability_release>)  (Implementation)


---
### bt\_pairability\_release\_bt<!-- {{#callable_declaration:bt_pairability_release_bt}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/common/bluetooth/pairability.h#L36>)

Decreases the reference count for Bluetooth Classic pairability.
- **Description**: Use this function to decrease the reference count for Bluetooth Classic pairability when it is no longer needed. This function should be called after a corresponding call to `bt_pairability_use_bt` to ensure proper management of pairability state. It is important to maintain a balance between calls to `bt_pairability_use_bt` and this function to avoid incorrect pairability states. The function assumes that the reference count is not zero when called.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`bt_pairability_release_bt`](<pairability.c.md#bt_pairability_release_bt>)  (Implementation)


---
### bt\_pairability\_release\_ble<!-- {{#callable_declaration:bt_pairability_release_ble}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/common/bluetooth/pairability.h#L39>)

Decreases the reference count for BLE pairability.
- **Description**: Use this function to decrease the reference count for Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) pairability. It must be called when BLE pairability is no longer needed, after a prior call to `bt_pairability_use_ble` or `bt_pairability_use_ble_for_period`. Ensure that the reference count is not zero before calling this function, as it asserts that the count is non-zero. This function helps manage the state of BLE discoverability and pairability by scheduling an evaluation of the current pairability status.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`bt_pairability_release_ble`](<pairability.c.md#bt_pairability_release_ble>)  (Implementation)

### bt\_pairability\_init<!-- {{#callable_declaration:bt_pairability_init}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/common/bluetooth/pairability.h#L45>)

Initializes Bluetooth pairability settings.
- **Description**: Use this function to initialize the Bluetooth pairability settings of the system. It must be called before any other pairability functions to ensure the system is correctly set up to handle Bluetooth pairing requests. This function prepares the system to evaluate and update its pairability status based on bonding changes.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`bt_pairability_init`](<pairability.c.md#bt_pairability_init>)  (Implementation)
